Thread Logic is a puzzle game where you must remove all screws from a wooden board. Each screw follows a threaded path and can only be removed when fully unscrewed and unblocked. Plan your moves carefully - screws can block each other's paths! Complete all 5 levels with increasing complexity to master the mechanics. Score points for completing levels quickly and efficiently. Arrow Keys: Move cursor (and selected screw up/down along its path) Space: Select/confirm removal of screw Shift: Deselect current screw Enter: Start game Escape: Pause/unpause R: Restart to main menu Thread Logic

Thread Logic is a puzzle game where you must remove all screws from a wooden board. Each screw follows a threaded path and can only be removed when fully unscrewed and unblocked. Plan your moves carefully - screws can block each other's paths! Complete all 5 levels with increasing complexity to master the mechanics. Score points for completing levels quickly and efficiently.

Arrow Keys: Move cursor (and selected screw up/down along its path) | Space: Select/confirm removal of screw | Shift: Deselect current screw | Enter: Start game | Escape: Pause/unpause | R: Restart to main menu

<hard_constraints>

- Game phases: START→PLAYING (ENTER), PLAYING→GAME_OVER (win/lose), GAME_OVER→START (R), PLAYING↔PAUSED (ESC). Expose this in a variable called `gameState.gamePhase` where `gameState` is a global object accessible via `window.getGameState()`.

    - ESC (27)    – pause / unpause

<code_instructions>

  - Group related functionality together in meaningful modules
  - Proper imports:

```javascript
// Essential pattern (ALWAYS follow this)
```

  - Use a single `gameState` object to track the game's current data and status. Store all game data in the `gameState` object to be used by automated testing code to play-test the game.    

- p.logs is write-only. Do not reset it. We want to keep track of all the logs since the start of the p5.js game instance.
- Variables to store in the p.logs during gameplay:
  - In "game_info": Information about the game when there are changes in the game phases

Thread Logic

Thread Logic is a puzzle game where you must remove all screws from a wooden board. Each screw follows a threaded path and can only be removed when fully unscrewed and unblocked. Plan your moves carefully - screws can block each other's paths! Complete all 5 levels with increasing complexity to master the mechanics. Score points for completing levels quickly and efficiently.

Arrow Keys: Move cursor (and selected screw up/down along its path) | Space: Select/confirm removal of screw | Shift: Deselect current screw | Enter: Start game | Escape: Pause/unpause | R: Restart to main menu
